key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 dividend 44.0 yen/sh plus conservative recurring investment income; excludes one-time Alibaba mark gains
Dividend plus modest recurring investment income

open_questions: Confirm live TSE quote before sizing (Yahoo **¥5,918**, 2026-07-24).
Reconcile component uplift (**+¥2,000/sh**) to latest listed stake prices and segment fair values.
Review asset-backed financing terms (Arm, Alibaba forward, SoftBank Corp margin) for covenant and collateral triggers.
OpenAI **$30B** net commitment: funding source and impact on parent liquidity.
Promote any third-party source via `_system/frameworks/third_party_sources.md` before base IRR blend.
thesis: SoftBank Group Corp. (9984.T) is a **pure holding company** listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange. **[Fact]** It does not run a standalone operating business at the parent; economics flow through four reportable segments: **holdco investments** (listed and private stakes, including Alibaba marks), **SoftBank Vision Funds (SVF)**, **SoftBank Corp.** (Japanese telecom, majority-owned subsidiary), and **Arm Holdings** (semiconductor IP, listed stake). **[Fact]** FY2025 (ended 2025-03-31) consolidated parent owners equity was **¥11,561,541 million** on **1,452.982 million** average shares, or **¥7,957** book value per share. **[Fact]** Parent net profit was **¥1,153,332 million**, but holdco investment profit alone was **¥3,413,821 million**, driven largely by Alibaba fair-value marks rather than recurring cash. Return to shareholders today is mostly dividends (**¥44** per share full year FY2025) plus mark-driven equity, not steady parent-level free cash flow.
why_market_wrong: The stock at **¥5,918** (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-24) trades at a deep discount to both consolidated book (**¥7,957** per share) and our proof-backed component base of **¥9,432** per share. **[Inference]** Generalists anchor on volatile investment gains, Vision Fund losses, and headline leverage from **asset-backed financing** (Arm margin **¥1,258.5 billion**, Alibaba forward **¥997.8 billion**, SoftBank Corp margin **¥796 billion** as of FY2025) rather than look-through asset value. **[Inference]** The market may be over-discounting Arm royalties, SoftBank Corp dividend upstream, and eventual Alibaba monetization while under-weighting the OpenAI commitment (up to **$30 billion** net) and financing unwind risk. Mispricing is **asset-lens**: value sits in stakes and catalyst exits, not in normalized owner cash at spot.
